description: Provide low-latency, reliable collaborative presence for documents via PresenceTracker so peers can join/leave and focus documents in real time.
Offer local and remote document summarization (LocalSummarizer, RemoteSummarizer) with a pluggable Summarizer interface and a sensible default (getDefaultSummarizer).
Maintain durable, searchable document state with DocumentStore, indexing (InvertedIndex, PersistentSyncQueue) and a SyncEngine to keep peers consistent.
Support third‑party plugins through PluginHost and PluginContext while preventing plugins from introducing unsafe coupling (e.g. via __PLUGIN_USES_PLUGINCONTEXT sentinel).
Keep the signal-app module boundary (app) small and well-documented so module comprehension remains high (module confidence 95%).
Single-module architecture: signal-app owns domain entities (Summarizer, PresenceTracker, GraphBuilder, storage and sync services) and exposes contracts rather than separate microservices.
Pluggable summarization strategy: Summarizer interface with LocalSummarizer (pure) and RemoteSummarizer (network side effects) allows switching between local/remote models.
Event-driven persistence: StorageEventBus is used to attach validators and propagate storage events (StorageEventCreated/Updated/Deleted/Linked) across DocumentStore and consumers.
Sync/Indexing pipeline: PersistentSyncQueue and InvertedIndex form the durability + search indexing path; SyncEngine orchestrates peer synchronization.
Graph analysis subsystem: GraphBuilder provides buildGraph (async) and findClusters (async) to derive adjacency/cluster views used by features that navigate document relationships.

PresenceTracker.join(peerId, documentId?) has detected io side effects and returns PeerPresence; PresenceTracker.setValidator is recorded as pure — validator can be synchronous or async but setValidator itself doesn't perform IO.

createValidatorFromStore is intentionally removed/typed as never and documented as a migration sentinel; guidance points to StorageEventBus.attachDocumentValidatorFromEvents + PresenceTracker.setValidator as the supported migration path.
RemoteSummarizer and RemoteSummarizer.summarize are annotated with network side effects — remote calls can introduce latency and failure modes independent from LocalSummarizer.
Two low-confidence symbols detected: SyncQueue (confidence 45%) and __PLUGIN_USES_PLUGINCONTEXT (confidence 45%) — these are comprehension gaps that may hide runtime coupling or removed APIs.
Module boundary 'signal-app' contains 20 files and is the primary ownership surface (owners list includes GraphBuilder, PresenceTracker, LocalSummarizer, DocumentStore, SyncEngine, etc.).
GraphBuilder.buildGraph is high-confidence and asynchronous; GraphBuilder.findClusters is present but recently had refactoring/ID changes in the graph export — verify callers still exist.
Storage-related invariants exist: guard clauses, try/catch error boundaries and rate-limiting patterns are present (detected across several files), indicating defensive code around IO.
Lineage includes migration entries that specifically remove createValidatorFromStore and provide migration guidance — the codebase already contains the migration artifact and messaging for consumers.
Presence validation must not allow arbitrary sync-time IO to block the join path: validators supplied to PresenceTracker.setValidator may be async but should not make the realtime path unavailable.
DocumentStore and StorageEventBus must preserve event ordering for attachDocumentValidatorFromEvents consumers so presence and index invariants remain consistent.
Plugins must not bypass PluginContext safety; contracts exposed via PluginHost must enforce capability boundaries (no direct access to internal DocumentStore without explicit API).
Summarization must tolerate remote failures: RemoteSummarizer network failures should degrade to LocalSummarizer or return controlled errors (avoid unbounded retries blocking user flows).
SyncEngine + PersistentSyncQueue durability: queued sync entries must be persisted to survive process restarts to avoid data loss during peer sync.

If createValidatorFromStore is removed but some older plugins or deployments still expect it, presence checks for migrated documents will fail at runtime (test by enabling legacy plugin and attempting join/focus flows).
Relying on RemoteSummarizer in latency-sensitive UI paths will increase user-perceived latency and error rates; replacing long-running calls with LocalSummarizer should reduce tail latency.

Low-confidence SyncQueue symbol indicates either a stale API or poor type coverage — attempts to rely on SyncQueue implementation details will break across upgrades (validate with integration tests exercising queue persistence/cleanup).

If PluginContext assumptions are underspecified (see __PLUGIN_USES_PLUGINCONTEXT sentinel), third-party plugins may inadvertently access internal APIs leading to security or data-integrity issues (fuzz plugin APIs in CI).

GraphBuilder.findClusters may be unused or diverge from buildGraph results after refactors; features depending on clustering (UI visualizations, recommendations) will show stale/incorrect clusters unless tests cover both methods.

PresenceTracker.setValidator is documented as a pure setter (no IO) while presence validation in practice may require async/store-backed checks — tension between pure API surface and required async validation semantics.

The codebase purposely removed createValidatorFromStore (migration) to prevent new coupling, but lineage/migration notes and presence APIs still need a clear, testable replacement path (StorageEventBus.attachDocumentValidatorFromEvents) — risk of migration friction.

RemoteSummarizer introduces network side effects in a subsystem where LocalSummarizer is pure — this creates contradictory operational expectations for callers (some expect deterministic local behavior, others expect remote accuracy).

Plugin extensibility (PluginHost/PluginContext) aims for flexibility but the low-confidence marker __PLUGIN_USES_PLUGINCONTEXT suggests incomplete documentation or unsafe opt-ins, clashing with the stated constraint to prevent plugins from introducing coupling.
